questionsList.questionContent: According to Genesis 39:5, how did God bless Potiphar's household on account of Joseph?

answersList.value: The LORD blessed Potiphar's household and everything he had, in house and field
Potiphar's lands produced double harvests every season for seven years
Potiphar received great honor and gifts from Pharaoh on Joseph's account
Every member of Potiphar's family was kept in good health

questionsList.questionContent: In the cupbearer's dream, what did the three branches represent, according to Joseph's interpretation?

answersList.value: Three years of service before Pharaoh would review his case
Three days, after which Pharaoh would restore him to his former position
Three generations before his family would prosper again
Three harvests of the vine he would oversee for Pharaoh

answersList.value: Seven thin cows rising from the Nile
A scorching wind from the eastern desert
A great swarm of locusts from the south
Seven thin and scorched heads of grain

answersList.value: Gold, silver, and the finest linen cloth of Canaan
Twenty of their best goats and a jar of wine
Balm, honey, spices, myrrh, pistachio nuts, and almonds
Fine cloth, carved woodwork, and perfumed oil

questionsList.questionContent: What economic policy did Joseph implement during the severe famine to acquire all Egypt's land for Pharaoh?

answersList.value: He bought all Egypt's land in exchange for grain, then required one-fifth of every crop for Pharaoh
He taxed all merchants and foreign caravans passing through Egypt's territory
He required foreign nations to surrender their gold and silver reserves in exchange for Egyptian grain
He redistributed all of Egypt's farmland equally to feed the starving population

questionsList.questionContent: What did Joseph make the Israelites swear an oath about before his death?

answersList.value: To honor his name and teach it to every future generation
To carry his bones out of Egypt when God brought them to the promised land
To bury him beside his father Jacob in Canaan within one year of his death
To keep his embalmed body in a place of honor in the sanctuary

The greatest priests would come from Judah's line alone

Two rivers; the first was the Euphrates

questionsList.questionContent: What precise argument did the serpent use to convince Eve that eating the fruit would not cause death?

answersList.value: "God knows this fruit contains immortality and does not want you to have it"
"God knows that when you eat it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil"
"The fruit only bestows wisdom, and God has hidden it from you unjustly"

Seth built a city on a wide plain and named it after his son Enosh
Adam built a settlement by the river and called it the City of Nod

Lamech was Noah's father

answersList.value: "And Enoch feared the LORD above all men of his generation"

"Enoch was righteous and blameless before the LORD"
"Enoch was more beloved by God than any who came before him"

To identify the nations promised to Abraham's descendants as their inheritance
To record the covenant nations that would stand at the end of days
To name the nations that would be judged for their wickedness in Canaan

A poetic refrain summarizing the events that just occurred
A structural heading that divides the book into its major sections
